_meta.contact: Grand Haven Charter Township Community Development — ghtmi.gov
_meta.notes: Grand Haven Charter Township is separate from Grand Haven City. Official zone codes: AG, RP, RR, R-1, R-2, R-3, R-4, PUD, C-1, C-2, I-1. Codes like SFR, RG1, RG2, RG3, SAO, IL-O appearing in Ottawa County GIS for this bounding box belong to neighboring municipalities (Spring Lake, Ferrysburg, Grand Haven City) — they are excluded here. All density figures derived from minimum lot area per DU; no explicit units/acre caps stated. Large-scale rule: 9+ dwelling units (8+ units for apartments) in RR/R-1/R-2 MUST be developed as a PUD (Section 14.01).

AG.label: Agricultural District
AG.density_basis: 20-acre minimum lot area = ~0.05 u/ac theoretical. Residential is incidental to agricultural operations only — not a primary residential district. Treated as 0 for development screening.
AG.notes: Agriculture, greenhouses, farm labor housing, kennels, roadside stands by right. No residential development pathway to ≥3 u/ac.

RP.label: Rural Preserve District
RP.density_basis: 5-acre minimum lot = ~0.2 u/ac. No municipal water/sewer expected. No path to ≥3 u/ac.
RP.notes: Transitional district between AG and residential. Single-family only. No municipal infrastructure. No path to ≥3 u/ac.

RR.label: Rural Residential District
RR.density_basis: 45,000 sq ft min lot = ~0.97 u/ac. PUD density bonus max +25% → ~1.2 u/ac — still below 3 u/ac threshold. No path to ≥3 u/ac.
RR.notes: Single-family only. 9+ unit developments MUST proceed as PUD (Section 14.01). PUD density bonus cannot bring RR to ≥3 u/ac — eliminated from results. Two-family not listed as SLU in RR.

R-1.label: Single Family Residential
R-1.density_basis: 15,000 sq ft min lot = ~2.9 u/ac (floor → 2). PUD density bonus up to 25% → ~3.6 u/ac on ≥5 acre sites. PUD pathway clears ≥3 u/ac threshold.
R-1.notes: Single-family by right. Two-family as Special Land Use only (Section 12.50) — must connect to municipal water + sewer, exterior must resemble SFR. PUD mandatory for 9+ units (Section 14.01). PUD density bonus on ≥5 ac can reach ~3.6 u/ac.

R-2.label: Single Family and Two-Family Residential
R-2.density_basis: 13,000 sq ft min lot = ~3.35 u/ac (floor → 3). Duplexes by right. ≥3 u/ac by right with sewer.
R-2.notes: Single-family and two-family by right. Two-family must meet Section 12.50 requirements (municipal water + sewer, SFR exterior appearance, side setbacks of 35 ft each / 75 ft combined). 9+ unit developments MUST proceed as PUD (Section 14.01).

R-3.label: Multiple Family Residential
R-3.density_basis: Explicitly stated: 1 dwelling unit per 3,250 sq ft of gross lot area = ~13.4 u/ac. Floor → 13.
R-3.notes: Multifamily by right. Explicit density formula: 1 unit per 3,250 sqft gross lot area. Single-family, two-family, and multifamily all permitted. Municipal water and sewer required for MF.

R-4.label: Manufactured Housing Park District
R-4.density_basis: 5,000 sq ft per unit = ~8.7 u/ac (floor → 8). Manufactured housing parks under Michigan Mobile Home Commission Act.
R-4.notes: Manufactured housing parks only. State-regulated per Michigan Mobile Home Commission Act. Not a conventional multifamily development pathway. Front setback of 100 ft is from public ROW.

PUD.label: Planned Unit Development District
PUD.density_basis: PUD is a zone district applied after approval — density is site-specific based on parallel plan plus density bonus. Estimate of 12 u/ac reflects a typical mixed residential PUD; actual density requires review of the specific PUD agreement.
PUD.notes: PUD parcels are already rezoned — density was set during PUD approval. Actual density, uses, and dimensional standards are governed by the recorded PUD Agreement, not standard zoning tables. Verify with Township.

C-1.label: Neighborhood Commercial District
C-1.density_basis: Residential on upper floors as Special Land Use only. No standalone residential density standard. Treated as 0 for screening.
C-1.notes: Neighborhood-scale commercial. Residential on upper floors as SLU only — not a primary residential development pathway.

C-2.label: Regional Commercial District
C-2.density_basis: Residential on upper floors as Special Land Use only. No standalone residential density standard. Treated as 0 for screening.
C-2.notes: Regional-scale commercial along US-31 corridor. Residential on upper floors as SLU only. US-31 Overlay (Chapter 8) may apply — 100 ft front setback along US-31.

pd_process.section: N/A — Grand Haven Township does not have a separate PD zone. PUD (Chapter 14) is the equivalent mechanism.
pd_process.notes: Grand Haven Township uses PUD as both a zone district (applied after approval) and a Special Land Use process for large-scale developments in RR/R-1/R-2. See prd_special_use section.

prd_special_use.label: Planned Unit Development (PUD)
prd_special_use.approval_required: Planning Commission (recommendation) + Township Board (final approval) + recorded PUD Agreement

RR.basis: Base 0.97 u/ac × 1.25 max SF bonus = 1.2 u/ac — below 3 u/ac threshold

R-1.basis: Base 2.9 u/ac × 1.25 max SF bonus = 3.6 u/ac — clears ≥3 u/ac threshold

R-2.basis: Base 3.35 u/ac × 1.25 max SF bonus = 4.2 u/ac

R-3.basis: Base 13.4 u/ac × 1.30 max MF bonus = 17.4 u/ac

sf_density_bonus.notes: Additional 10/20/30% bonus for LEED/EnergyStar, Missing Middle housing, or voluntary affordable set-asides

prd_special_use.large_scale_rule: 9+ dwelling units (8+ apartment units) in RR/R-1/R-2 MUST proceed as PUD (Section 14.01)
qualifying_conditions: Mixed uses in a single development
Site contains natural features of significance exceeding 25% of site area
Site configuration makes standard zoning impractical
prd_special_use.notes: PUD base density cannot exceed underlying zone parallel plan. Density bonus for open space preservation beyond minimums. R-1 parcels ≥5 acres can reach 3.6 u/ac via PUD with max SF bonus. RR cannot reach ≥3 u/ac even with max bonus.

affordable_housing.notes: No mandatory inclusionary zoning. Voluntary affordable housing set-asides earn additional PUD density bonus: 10% ALICE-level units = 10% bonus; 20% units below 80% ALICE = 30% bonus. Not a requirement for PUD approval.
